text.title: Client Style Guide Importer Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Client Style Guide Importer application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Extractor MUST respect heading structure (H1/H2/H3) when chunking; each chunk inherits the section path as a tag. Flat 1000-char chunks are rejected.
rule.rationale: Style guides are hierarchical; section path is the only reliable provenance signal when a rule fires at validation.

rule.statement: Only sentences matching the imperative-clause pattern (MUST / SHOULD / MUST NOT / SHOULD NOT) or the bullet/list normative form are emitted as candidate rules. Free prose is held in a 'context' bucket, not the rule output.
rule.rationale: Style guides include rationale and history that are not rules; mixing them with rules pollutes the agent context window.

rule.statement: Every machine-extracted candidate rule MUST be reviewed by a human (engagement lead or client owner) before it lands in `conventions.yaml`. Auto-merge is rejected.
rule.rationale: LLM extraction has false-positive imperative detection (advice phrased as 'must'); human review is the only viable filter at current model quality.

rule.statement: After emitting `conventions.yaml`, the pipeline MUST regenerate Markdown from the YAML and diff it against the original. Drift > 5 lines per page = re-extract.
rule.rationale: Round-trip detects extractor regressions and lets the client trust the file as a faithful summary.

rule.statement: Every emitted rule MUST carry a `source.section` and `source.line` field pointing back to the original guide. Un-tagged rules are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without source tags, the client cannot answer 'where did this come from' and the file becomes folklore on the next engagement.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
